title: Visual mental imagery: Evidence for a heterarchical neural architecture.

assets.abstract: Theories of Visual Mental Imagery (VMI) emphasize the processes of retrieval, modification, and recombination of sensory information from long-term memory. Yet, only few studies have focused on the behavioral mechanisms and neural correlates supporting VMI of stimuli from different semantic domains. Therefore, we currently have a limited understanding of how the brain generates and maintains mental representations of colors, faces, shapes - to name a few. Such an undetermined scenario renders un

phase_mapping.bridge_rationale: Imaginal — mental imagery as real perception, imagination activating sensory cortices, aphantasia.
